root_cause: Webhook-dispatcher version 2026.03.03.2 reduced the outbound request timeout to 2 seconds, which was below the normal 2.3 to 2.4 second latency of the payroll-sync partner. Requests that previously succeeded began timing out locally, causing retries, duplicate work, and a rapidly growing delivery backlog.

details: Outbound timeout warnings all fail at roughly 2000ms and immediately requeue deliveries
After rollback, payroll-sync deliveries succeed with durations around 2417ms
partner_payroll_sync.probe_latency_p95_ms remains stable near 2.4s instead of showing a dependency outage
retry_rate_percent and queue_depth spike immediately after deploy, indicating local retry amplification rather than upstream unavailability
Commit 11c0d9a explicitly changed outbound webhook timeout behavior in the deployed version
The incident starts minutes after webhook-prod-3001 and resolves after rollback webhook-prod-3002

recommended_actions: Restore a timeout higher than normal partner latency and document per-partner timeout expectations
Alert when local client timeout settings drop below observed dependency latency percentiles
Add retry amplification guards so timeouts do not immediately create backlog explosions
expected_investigation_path: inspect webhook-dispatcher timeout and retry logs
compare queue depth, retry rate, and independent partner probe latency to distinguish partner outage from local timeout misconfiguration
review recent commits touching outbound HTTP client settings
validate the theory against deployment timing and successful post-rollback deliveries
